Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»‘Pensioner’ Chad Le Clos ready to give his all for South Africa

Chad Le Clos has picked up an injury ahead of the games and is the third-oldest male swimmer in Paris but he still believes he has a shot at gold.

Chad Le Clos touches down in Paris already the most decorated Olympian South Africa has ever produced but despite describing himself as a ‘pensioner’ and admitting to carrying an injury, he is upbeat about his chances of adding to his medal haul.

He is one of five South Africans to represent our country in forOlympic Gamesand his four medals (One gold and three silver) make him our most successful Olympian in history.

Le Clos will be the third oldest male swimmer to get into the Olympic pool in Paris but feels that he still has a shot at glory and hopes for a fifth medal.

On top of his Olympic credentials, Le Clos has an incredible record in other FINA events and has been particularly dominant in short course swimming down the years.

The 32-year-old takes to the water in the 100m butterfly and admits that he has a lot to do to keep up with the next generation of top swimmers.

Le Clos is racing hurt after picking up a muscular injury in training for the games, but refused to let that stand in the way of him turning out in Paris.

“I’m the third oldest male swimmer at these Games, so in those terms I’m something of a pensioner,” Le Clos toldTEAM SOUTH AFRICA.

“And in spite of picking up a shoulder tear in training, I’ve come to Paris believing I have a chance. I’ve raced with injuries before.”

Le Clos believes that if he can break through the heats and get himself into the semi-finals, he will have a shot at making a final where anything could happen.

The Olympic icon also serves as a leader in the team environment that the Games are known for.

“I think that the semi-final will be the harder race, because there will be 16 swimmers going for a place in the final. I’m hoping to get there and once I do I’ll be chasing another dream. I’m a warrior, a born racer.”

Le Clos downplayed the impact of his injury and said that he is in Paris to soak up that Olympic spirit one more time.

“Yes, I’ve had an injury the last two weeks. I went to the medical team as soon as I arrived from Montpellier and had it checked out. The first thing I did after that was head for the dining hall!

“I’m here to enjoy the experience, even if I don’t think it’s my last, and give of my best.”

title: Chad Le Clos ready to give his all for South Africa
author: James Richardson
description: Chad Le Clos has picked up an injury ahead of the games and is the third-oldest male swimmer in Paris but he believes he has a shot.
